result:
If I could change one thing about the world, I would instill in every person the capacity for and commitment to radical empathy. By this, I mean the ability to genuinely understand and share the feelings of another person, even if their experiences and beliefs are vastly different from your own. This isn't just feeling *for* someone, but truly feeling *with* them.

I believe that widespread radical empathy would be a catalyst for profound positive change. It would reduce conflict, foster greater understanding between cultures and communities, inspire more effective problem-solving (as solutions would be tailored to truly meet the needs of those affected), and ultimately create a more just and compassionate world for all.
